mainEntity.name: What is the professional tax rate in Tamil Nadu?

acceptedAnswer.text: Tamil Nadu professional tax maximum is Rs 2,500 per annum. Monthly deduction: Rs 208 (salary above Rs 21,000). Half-yearly payment for self-employed professionals. Rates are prescribed under the Tamil Nadu Tax on Professions, Trades, Callings and Employments Act, 1992.

mainEntity.name: Is professional tax registration mandatory for all employers in Chennai?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Every employer in Tamil Nadu with salaried employees must obtain PTEC (enrollment) and PTRC (registration for TDS) and deduct professional tax from employee salaries. Failure to register and deduct attracts penalties.

mainEntity.name: Can Virtual Auditor handle PT along with payroll processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. We integrate professional tax computation and deduction with our payroll processing service. PT is computed based on applicable slabs, deducted from salary, deposited, and reported — all as part of the monthly payroll cycle.

faqs.q: What is the professional tax rate in Tamil Nadu?
faqs.a: Tamil Nadu professional tax maximum is Rs 2,500 per annum. Monthly deduction: Rs 208 (salary above Rs 21,000). Half-yearly payment for self-employed professionals. Rates are prescribed under the Tamil Nadu Tax on Professions, Trades, Callings and Employments Act, 1992.

faqs.q: Is professional tax registration mandatory for all employers in Chennai?
faqs.a: Yes. Every employer in Tamil Nadu with salaried employees must obtain PTEC (enrollment) and PTRC (registration for TDS) and deduct professional tax from employee salaries. Failure to register and deduct attracts penalties.
